title: Warning: Prop className did not match.

messages.author: th0th
messages.body:
# Examples bug report

## Example name
[with-styled-components](https://github.com/zeit/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-styled-components)

## Describe the bug
Also posted here: #2538

Using `css` prop introduced with styled-components v4 causes `Warning: Prop className did not match.`.

## To Reproduce
Add any HTML element with `css` prop.

## Expected behavior
Correctly styled rendering on the server side without any warnings.

## Screenshots
![57497472-7b657c00-72e0-11e9-84c5-e7e5fa0d351c](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/698079/57632876-37f26280-75ab-11e9-91a2-a270d92f122d.png)

## System information
 - OS: Windows
 - Chrome
 - Version of Next.js: 8.1.0

## Additional context
Here is an example I created to demonstrate the issue: https://codesandbox.io/embed/jlwvwyy0ow

Open this and refresh once the building is done: https://jlwvwyy0ow.sse.codesandbox.io/

messages.author: TheDSCPL
messages.body:
Well,  actually I believe I've found a bug in the hot reload part of Next.JS's dev server.

I got the same warning again so I put a console.log in the `_default` function inside `babel-plugin-styled-components` module's `lib/index.js` file to see when the plugin was being used.

It turns out that the initial build runs the plugin but subsequent rebuilds (whenever a file is edited) don't rerun babel. Is this due to some kind of incremental build algorithm, @timneutkens?

Anyway, I'm not sure of what is causing the problem but it seems to be a problem during the rebuild process.

messages.author: timneutkens
messages.body: We cache Babel output and only rebuild the parts that cache, if you add a plugin the plugin itself is executed on bootup and the visitors will be executed when you change files.

messages.body:
So I managed to fix this in my situation.. I'm just running React 16.12.0, Next 9.2.1 and Material 4.9.1. Much like everyone else here I found that it was fine on the initial save, however when reloading in browser it was throwing an error where the class names were different. I found a fix for now.

```
$ npm install --save-dev babel-plugin-styled-components
$ touch .babelrc
```
Paste the following into your .babelrc file - 

```
{
    "plugins": [
      ["styled-components", { "ssr": true, "displayName": true, "preprocess": false } ],
    ],
    "presets": ["next/babel"]
}
```

Just re-run your dev env and the problem went away. Apologies if I've done something stupid here, I'm still relatively new to next.js & react!

messages.author: github0013
messages.body:
My situation was something like this.

1. It is a dynamic component
2. When user is NOT logged in, display gray color avatar
3. when user is logged in, display avatar in a color
4. I control the color by adding a className "logged-in"

https://github.com/zeit/next.js/issues/7322#issuecomment-612965316
Maybe for this reason, the displayed avatar was in gray color even the user was logged in on a page refresh/reload. (while editing in dev mode, hot reload kinda picks up and was showing colored avatar, but it was still buggy)

I then did something like this

```tsx

  const [loggedIn, setLoggedIn] = React.useState(false)
  React.useEffect(() => {
    setLoggedIn(check_if_user_is_signed_in())
  }, [check_if_user_is_signed_in()])

  if (loggedIn) {
    return (
      <Link href="/users">
        <IconButton>
          <Avatar className="user logged-in" />
        </IconButton>
      </Link>
    )
  }

  return (
    <Link href="/login">
      <IconButton>
        <Avatar className="user" />
      </IconButton>
    </Link>
  )

```

and it seems to work fine.

> [#12338 (comment)](https://github.com/zeit/next.js/discussions/12338#discussioncomment-8285)

Yes, I just discovered this method and it works in my case as my components has data to load on client side. option SSR needs to be false so that component is loaded on the client side dynamically and is not SSR. 
In my case, the slider components has classes that are initialized and added to elements on client side after the document window is ready. So, the solution will work in all similar cases where the classes are initialized and added on the client side. See my code below.

`
const AGSlider = dynamic(
  () => import('../components/slider'),
  { ssr: false, loading: () => <Loader /> }
)
`

messages.author: timneutkens
messages.body:
If you're running into this and using a CSS-in-JS library like styled-components / emotion / Material UI make sure you configure it correctly. Here's the examples:
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-styled-components
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-emotion
- https://github.com/mui-org/material-ui/tree/master/examples/nextjs

---

> It has been 2 years since this issue has been raised and still this exists. Nextjs shows very little attention to users concerns which makes this library unreliable. I am already looking into alternatives.

Let's break down the things you're claiming:

> It has been 2 years since this issue has been raised and still this exists.

The warning you're seeing is a React hydration warning. The warning shows when the pre-rendered (SSR or SSG) response differs from the client-side React tree. In this case the className generated during pre-rendering is different from the one client-side. Generally this is caused by not configuring the CSS-in-JS library for pre-rendering, e.g. in case of styled-components you have to add this bit: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/examples/with-styled-components/pages/_document.js#L5-L29 which allows for tracking what styled-components were rendered and publishing those into a `<style>` tag server-side.

> Nextjs shows very little attention to users concerns which makes this library unreliable

- Next.js is being maintained by 5 people full-time and many external collaborators/contributors. It's currently almost 3.5 years old and has been continuously maintained.
- Questions are being asked and answered almost non-stop every day on https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions by both community members and the core team.
Furthermore we continuously talk to users.
- New releases are being pushed out every few days on a nightly build (canary): https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases
- We have an extensive test suite that covers all features and all new features / fixes are accompanied by integration tests. Note that these tests run against the full Next.js CLI and don't mock anything. https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/test/integration
- Next.js is being used by over 72K sites on the internet [source](https://trends.builtwith.com/framework/Next.js) and there's even more usage internally at companies. This includes the world's largest known web properties like qq.com (4th largest website of the world according to [Alexa](https://www.alexa.com/topsites) (bigger than Facebook)), TikTok, Twitch, and Hulu. All driving their main web experience.
- We're working with the Google Chrome team and Facebook's React team on Next.js and tackling a lot of hard problems related to performance (e.g. [Granular Chunking](https://nextjs.org/blog/next-9-2#improved-code-splitting-strategy)), development experience (e.g. [Fast Refresh](https://nextjs.org/blog/next-9-4#fast-refresh)). More info: https://youtu.be/UD98x-2mido?t=1643
- The reception of every 9.x release (9.0, 9.1, 9.2, 9.3, 9.4) has been overwhelmingly positive and the continuous feedback we've had has been that with every release users can remove more of their custom code in favor of built-in reliable solutions.

The next.js example for styled-components only says `"ssr": true` in the babel config. The secret ingredient for me was to add `"displayName": true` as well. 

```
{
  "presets": ["next/babel"],
  "plugins": [["styled-components", { "ssr": true, "displayName": true }]]
}
```

messages.author: fultonm
messages.body:
> Running into this issue as well, but am not using styled components. The reason I am running in to it is because I have dynamic styles based on the selection of jump links eg "/#description".
> 
> 
> 
> Not sure of a good fix for it though.

I was also getting this error with dynamic class names and not using styled components. It turns out Next.js will always do Server Side Generation and expects the first render on the client to agree with the SSG version. Subsequent renders are then allowed change the DOM to take into account client side state per my cursory understanding. There are different things you can do to ensure the first render matches what the server would generate (whatever it would generate without access to localStorage or the client's state container.) since the hashtag portion of jump links aren't sent to the server, and only interpreted by the browser, you may need to use a workaround to have the server be aware of it.

It was with this error I realized the always-on server side generation aspect was not compatible/logical with the design of my app and swapped to OG React. If this error is just for a page or two it might make sense to do the workarounds.

Just based on what I picked up before switching. Could be wrong. Hope this helps.

messages.author: christineurban
messages.body:
OMFG I finally fixed this after trying all the things and ignoring for months then coming back to try again over and over 🤯 🙌🏽  Note I'm using Next.js with Nx monorepo.
- drop this in ALL of the `.babelrc`s (in libs and apps) and the root`babel.config.json`:
```
"plugins": [
    ["styled-components", { "ssr": true, "displayName": true }]
  ]
```
- I left the `presets` the same in each babel config as they were all different and that didn't seem to affect anything
- **KEY CHANGE THAT MADE THIS WORK (for me):**   I deleted the root `dist` folder, since the project uses `next.config.js` instead of `.next`. I would think if your project uses a `build` folder you could delete that and have the same effect.
- Restart server and refresh pages over and over
- NO CLASS NAME MISMATCH 🥳 
- This also fixed my `displayName` issue where it was showing `sc-blahblah` and now it shows `Heading-blahblah`

messages.author: jzzfs
messages.body:
You can always resort to [`suppressHydrationWarning`](https://reactjs.org/docs/dom-elements.html#suppresshydrationwarning). Quoting react docs:

> If you set `suppressHydrationWarning` to `true`, React will not warn you about mismatches in the attributes and the content of that element. It only works one level deep, and is intended to be used as an escape hatch.

---

I recently had an issue w/ decoding a [`blurhash`](https://github.com/woltapp/blurhash/tree/master/TypeScript) to be used in a `next/image`. It turns out, [`node-canvas`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/canvas) (SSR) interprets an `Uint8ClampedArray` slightly differently than the Web API canvas which leads to different `blurDataURL`s.

Long story short, suppressing the warning did the trick:
```tsx
<Image
    suppressHydrationWarning={true}
   ...
/>
```

messages.body:
> Hi guys, I've tried so many solutions in this thread these couple few days, still get this warning. After some experiments, I confirmed that warnings are all came from my custom styled [framer-motion](https://github.com/framer/motion) components. > > So I changed the way to import those motion components by using [next/dynamic](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/dynamic-import) (with No SSR) and finally fixed this :D! > > example: > > ```js > // /pages/index.js > > import dynamic from 'next/dynamic' > > const CircleButtonWithNoSSR = dynamic(() => import('../components/Buttons/CircleButton'), { ssr: false }) > > const HomePage = () => { > return ( >

Home Page Title

> > ) > } > > export default HomePage > ``` > > ```js > // /components/Buttons/CircleButton.js > > import styled from 'styled-components' > import { motion } from 'framer-motion' > > const CircleButton = styled(motion.button)` > color: ${({ color }) => color ? color : 'red'}; > ` > > export default CircleButton > ``` Thank you so much! This fix worked for '@mui/styles/styled' too!
